reasoning: Duke's AdjEM (37.3) leads St. John's (25.7) by 11.6 points. Pitino's elite tactical mind and St. John's defense (AdjD 94.1) make this competitive, but Duke's generational offense (AdjO 128.1) creates too much scoring volume. Duke's archetypes (2024 Purdue, 2025 Florida) project championship-level performance.

UConn's AdjEM (28.1) edges Michigan State's (26.7) by 1.4 points. Both are elite defensive teams. Hurley's 15-5 tournament record with 2 titles and UConn's championship pedigree edge Izzo's experience in a game that comes down to final possessions.

Florida's AdjEM (33.8) holds a 5.7-point edge over Vanderbilt's (28.1). Both SEC teams with explosive offenses, but Florida's defense (AdjD 92.3 vs 99.3) is 7 points better — the clear separator. In an SEC rivalry Sweet 16, the team that gets stops advances.

Houston (AdjEM 33.0) vs Illinois (33.7) — essentially identical elite teams. Houston's historically elite defense (AdjD 92.4) is built to slow Illinois' high-volume offense (AdjO 131.9). Sampson's 31-21 tournament record with 4 FFs provides the March experience edge. Houston's defensive identity is specifically designed for opponents like Illinois.

Arizona's AdjEM (35.5) is 9.2 points above Arkansas's (26.3). Both play fast, but Arizona is more efficient on both ends. Arizona's defense (AdjD 91.4 vs 101.7) is the decisive edge. Calipari's pedigree keeps it competitive, but the Big 12 champion is the better team.

Purdue's AdjEM (33.0) holds a 6.7-point edge over Gonzaga's (26.3). Nation-best offense (AdjO 133.3) overwhelms even Gonzaga's solid defense (AdjD 94.0). With Huff potentially back but at limited capacity, Gonzaga's depth is still compromised. Painter's Big Ten champions advance.

Michigan's AdjEM (36.6) is 9.9 points above Alabama's (26.7). Michigan's elite defense (AdjD 91.0) is built to slow Alabama's blazing tempo. Alabama's explosive offense keeps it competitive, but Michigan's complete two-way profile is too balanced.

Iowa State's AdjEM (31.1) edges Virginia's (26.4) by 4.7 points. Both elite defensive teams at slow tempos, but Iowa State's offensive efficiency (AdjO 123.8 vs 122.2) creates the margin. Otzelberger's 9-5 record edges Sanchez's first tournament.
